titlestmt.titleproper: Bowen, Ill. Bowen Congregational Church records, 1856-1944.
titlestmt.author: Adrianna Keller-Wyman

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday May 8, 2026 at 14:58

did.unittitle: Bowen, Ill. Bowen Congregational Church records, 1856-1944.
did.abstract: The First Congregational Church of Bowen was organized on May 24th, 1856, as the Cedron Church of Hancock County. Reverend Samuel Dilley served as its first pastor. Before its dissolution in 1944, it was known as the First Congregational Church of Chili, the Bowensburg Congregational Church, and the Bowen Congregational Church. This collection includes two volumes of church records from 1856 to 1943 in addition to records regarding its 1944 dissolution.

corpname.part: First Congregational Church of Chili (Chili, Ill.)

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to this collection is restricted and open to the public.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on use
userestrict.p: Items in this collection are subject to U.S. Copyright Law. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine the copyright status of collection items and to secure any permissions necessary for their reproduction and publication. Requests for permission to publish material must be discussed with the archivist or librarian.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], in the Bowen, Ill. First Congregational Church records, 1856-1944, RG5229. The Congregational Library & Archives, Boston, MA.

acqinfo.head: Acquisition information
acqinfo.p: Materials were donated to the Congregational Library & Archives by the Chicago Theological Center in April 2014; accession number 2014-76.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was processed by Adrianna Keller-Wyman, April 2026, according to the rules of Describing Archives: A Content Standard.

bioghist.head: Historical Note
bioghist.p: The First Congregational Church of Bowen was organized on May 24th, 1856, as the Cedron Church of Hancock County and admitted its first members on May 1st, 1857. Reverend Samuel Dilley was appointed as pastor of the church on April 10th, 1858. Under his leadership, the church's name was changed to the First Congregational Church of Chili on March 2nd, 1863. Reverend Samuel Dilley resigned on December 6th, 1863, although he returned as pastor on January 1st, 1870.

On June 21st, the church moved into a new meeting house in Bowen, Illinois, which was known as Bowensburg, Illinois at the time. The name of the church was changed once again to the Bowensburg Congregational Church on April 19th, 1869. Reverend T.G. Owens was appointed as pastor on November 1st, 1873.
The church had another name change to the Bowen Congregational Church, most likely in or after 1887 when the town's name changed from Bowensburg to Bowen. In 1944, the church dissolved.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains two volumes of church records from 1856 to 1943, including working and absentee rolls, church history, resolutions of respect, baptismal records, ministry staff records, meeting minutes, and financial records. It also includes meeting minutes, newspaper clippings, correspondence, and an order of worship regarding the 1944 dissolution of the church.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Records in this collection have been arranged chronologically.

corpname.part: First Congregational Church of Chili (Chili, Ill.)
